zh.lifeSummary: 當William Barbee於1607年出生在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州米德萊可時，他的父親John Barbee20歲，母親Anne Miller27歲。他於1638年之前與Elizabeth Beaumont在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州阿可馬可米德萊可結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。他去世於1720年在他的家鄉享年113歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当William Barbee于1607年出生在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州米德萊可时，他的父亲John Barbee20岁，母亲Anne Miller27岁。他于1638年之前与Elizabeth Beaumont在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州阿可馬可米德萊可结婚。他们至少是1个女儿的父母。他去世于1720年在他的家乡享年113岁。

nameMeanings.name: Barbee
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of French Huguenot origin): Anglicized form of French Barbé (see Barbe ).
Americanized form of French Barbier and possibly also of Barbé (compare 2 above).

variants.name: Barby
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Merwin, Otto, Wolfgang.  French Celestine, Pierre.
German: habitational name from a place so named in Saxony-Anhalt.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.description: The Virginia Company founded the first permanent English colony at Jamestown in 1607. One of the original 13 states (it entered the Union in 1788). Virginia holds an important place in American history, as it was home to many of the founding fathers, including George Washington, Thomas Jefferson, James Madison, George Mason, and Patrick Henry. Four of Americas first five presidents were Virginians. During the Civil War, Richmond, Virginias capital, was the capital of the Confederacy. Today, Virginia is a popular tourist spot where people can visit historic places such as Alexandria, Williamsburg, and Mount Vernon, George Washingtons estate.

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

historicEvents.title: Glorious Revolution
historicEvents.description: The Glorious Revolution brought the downfall of Catholic King James II and the reign of his Protestant daughter Mary and her Dutch husband, William III of Orange.

historicEvents.title: South Sea Bubble
historicEvents.description: The South Sea Bubble Bill was passed by the House of Lords in 1720. This allowed the South Sea company to monopolize trade with South America. The company underwrote the English National Debt which promised 5% interest from the government. As shares rose exponentially, many companies were created and many fortunes were made. The stocks crashed and many people lost their money which caused them to become destitute overnight and suicide was common.  Robert Walpole took charge of the South Sea Bubble Financial Crisis by dividing the national debt between the Bank of England, the Treasury, and the Sinking Fund.

en.lifeSummary: When William Barbee was born in 1607, in Middlesex, Virginia, British Colonial America, his father, John Barbee, was 20 and his mother, Anne Miller, was 27. He married Elizabeth Beaumont before 1638, in Middlesex, Accomack, Virginia, British Colonial America. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. He died in 1720, in his hometown, at the age of 113.

ko.lifeSummary: William Barbee(이)가 1607년 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 버지니아 주 미들레세스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John Barbee(은)는 20세였고, 그의 어머니 Anne Miller(은)는 27세였다. 그는 1638년 이전 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 버지니아 주 악코맥 미들레세스에서 Elizabeth Beaumont(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1720년 그의 고향에서 113세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.Christ Church, Middlesex, Virginia, Brit: краист чурч, миддлесекс, Виргиния, бритиш колоняль амирика
ru.lifeSummary: Когда William Barbee родился в 1607 г. в местности миддлесекс, Виргиния, бритиш колоняль амирика, его отцу по имени John Barbee было 20 лет, а матери, которую звали Anne Miller, 27. Он женился на женщине по имени Elizabeth Beaumont перед 1638 г. в местности миддлесекс, аккомакк, Виргиния, бритиш колоняль амирика. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Он умер в 1720 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 113 лет.
